points: History is the study of the human past, involving experts like archaeologists, anthropologists, and geologists who analyze material remains, fossils, and cultures.

Time is measured using the Gregorian calendar with BCE (Before Common Era) and CE (Common Era); there is no year zero, so calculating years between BCE and CE dates requires adding them and subtracting one.
A timeline is a visual tool that arranges events in chronological order, helping us understand sequences like which event occurred before another.

Sources of history are classified into archaeological sources (material remains like tools, pottery, monuments, and bones) and literary sources (written texts like the Vedas, travelogues, and historical chronicles).
Oral sources, such as folklore and genealogies, and modern scientific aids, including genetics and climate studies, also contribute to reconstructing the past.
Historians use multiple sources to piece together history like a jigsaw puzzle, sometimes encountering contradictions that require careful judgment.
Modern humans (Homo sapiens) emerged about 300,000 years ago; early humans were hunter-gatherers who lived in caves, used stone tools, created rock art, and had beliefs about nature and afterlife.
The end of the last Ice Age around 12,000 years ago led to warmer climates, enabling the development of agriculture, domestication of animals, and the growth of settled communities.
Settlements grew from hamlets to villages and then to towns, with new technologies like pottery and metallurgy (copper and iron) emerging, setting the stage for the rise of civilization.
